qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SQL Injection in AJAX search via unsanitized term parameter across module search files
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SQL injection via the `term` GET parameter in `ajax_search.php` with LIKE clause concatenation across module search files. The advisory describes SQL injection via the `id_records[]` POST array parameter in `/modules/scadenzario/bulk.php` with IN clause injection. These are different endpoints, different parameters, different injection contexts (LIKE vs IN), different vulnerable code locations, and different root causes (HTMLPurifier not preventing SQLi vs array_clean not validating types). Same broad CWE-89 class but distinct vulnerable code paths.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes Error-Based SQL Injection via the `id_records[]` POST array in the Scadenzario bulk operations module at `/modules/scadenzario/bulk.php` (IN clause injection). The only qualifying finding, `sql_injection_ajax_search`, describes a different SQL injection via the `term` GET parameter in `ajax_search.php` (LIKE clause injection) — different endpoint, parameter, injection context, vulnerable code, and root cause.
evidence: Advisory identifies /modules/scadenzario/bulk.php line 88 with id_records[] in IN clause as the vulnerable code
sql_injection_ajax_search targets ajax_search.php with term parameter in LIKE clauses across module search files — different code path
